headline: Cyclone Idai makes landfall near Beira, Mozambique
summary: On 14 March 2019 at 22:00 UTC, Cyclone Idai made landfall near the city of Beira in Mozambique, at latitude ‑19.843 and longitude 34.839. The system is recorded with a severity rating of 0.85. The cyclone brought a combination of storm surge and prolonged river flooding. Approximately 600,000 people are estimated to be exposed, including the city of Beira, which has a population of about 533,000. The medium‑density urban area faces risks from both coastal surge and inland flood waters. Current information does not include the extent of flooding, damage to housing or infrastructure, or the number of people displaced. Further details on emergency response capacity and ongoing weather trends are also lacking. Information on the status of power, water, and health services is not yet available. The timing and magnitude of additional rainfall remain uncertain.
confirmed_facts: Cyclone Idai made landfall near Beira, Mozambique on 14 March 2019 at 22:00 UTC (lat -19.843, lon 34.839).
The cyclone is assigned a severity rating of 0.85.
An estimated 600,000 people are exposed, including the city of Beira with a population of 533,000.
The event is expected to produce storm surge and prolonged river flooding after landfall.
key_unknowns: Extent and depth of flooding in affected areas.
Number of people displaced, injured, or deceased.
Damage to housing, infrastructure, and critical services such as power, water, and health facilities.
Current status and capacity of emergency response and aid delivery.
watch_indicators: Rising river levels or new flood warnings in the Beira basin.
Reports of additional storm surge or coastal inundation.
Disruption or loss of power, water, or health services.
